name: TopbarNavigation
description: The full top navigation bar — brand logo (real Placeholder asset), a horizontal main-nav item list with a real active-item state, an optional inline search field (or a compact search icon button when hidden), quick-action + notification icon buttons, an "Others" overflow dropdown for extra nav items, and a TopbarUserProfile chip.
sourceNote: Ported from Figma node 3814:25274 (Topbar [Navigation]). Assembled entirely from the Topbar Components family (TopbarItem, TopbarUserProfile, TopbarIconButton) plus the existing DropdownItem and PlaceholderBrand pieces, rather than re-implementing their internals. Figma's rigid `product: 'HR Management' | 'Finance & Banking'` variant enum (two hardcoded nav configurations) was collapsed into a plain `items`/`moreItems` prop pair, matching the project's established pattern of trading Figma enums for composable props (e.g. SidebarNavigation's `mainItems`). The trailing "Others" chevron — static/decorative in the Figma source — was made genuinely functional: it's a real overflow dropdown (fixed-position panel + click-outside-to-close, the same technique introduced for HeaderCard's workspace switcher) listing `moreItems`, not a dead-end affordance. Third component filed under 'Product Components > Navigation', alongside SidebarNavigation and the Sidebar/Topbar Components primitives it's built from.

items.type: { key: string, label: string, icon: string }[]

moreItems.type: { key: string, label: string, icon: string }[]
